description: In the first ever session on a new machine, the user sets a durable preference: notifications timezone PST. A Hermes-style persistence path incorrectly treats the flush cursor as already satisfied (0===0 no-op), so the second and third sessions never observe a write barrier even though the preference should exist. Later sessions only contain unrelated work. The probe must still return PST by relying on durable memory rather than recent chat.

messages.content: First session on this laptop after imaging it. Please remember this permanently: for all scheduling and notification summaries, use my timezone PST. I'll build workflows on top of that assumption.
Preference stored. I'll treat PST as your canonical timezone for scheduling and notification summaries going forward.
Morning churn: can you draft a terse stand-up update for me based on yesterday's notes file? No calendar math needed.
Draft stand-up: shipped Hermes reconnect hardening, triaged two flaky integration tests, today focusing on bridge rollout checklist and CI stabilization.
Unrelated polish: rewrite this paragraph to sound less robotic but keep the technical claims intact. [paste omitted]
Here's a tighter version that keeps the latency and reliability numbers but reads more like a human release note.
I need a quick rubric for reviewing external PRs to our OpenClaw plugin—security, tests, docs—in bullet form.
Security: least privilege, secret handling, supply chain. Tests: contract tests for tool schemas, negative paths. Docs: upgrade notes, config examples, failure modes. Ship only with rollback story.
Noise control question: what's a sane default max tokens for summarizing long incident threads without losing root cause?
Start around 1.5k–2k tokens for a first pass summary with explicit bullets for timeline, blast radius, mitigations, and unknowns; escalate only if the user asks for depth.
Before we plan weekly reminders, what timezone should you assume for my notification summaries?

probe.prompt: Before we plan weekly reminders, what timezone should you assume for my notification summaries?

interference.description: Models the Hermes/OpenClaw bug class where a fresh session's first flush compares against an uninitialized cursor (0===0) and skips writing, so later sessions have no transcript anchor for PST even though the user explicitly requested durable storage.
Sessions 2–5 never restate the timezone, tempting systems that only read the local window to hallucinate UTC or the host default.
